title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During afternoon tea, Campbell arrives and is surprised by Emma, who has made him a birthday cake. Miss Marple, who is recovering from a cold, joins the group. During the meal, Mr. Cruncher makes jokes about poisoned fish paste, prompting Cecil to demonstrate the food's safety by swallowing a sandwich whole. Miss Marple then eats a sandwich but begins to choke on a fish bone. Campbell uses tweezers to examine her throat with professional skill.

When Mrs. McGillicuddy enters, she sees the scene and exclaims that Campbell is the man she saw on the train. Miss Marple uses this moment to trap Campbell, claiming Mrs. McGillicuddy is an eyewitness to his murder of a woman on a parallel train. Although Campbell attempts to intimidate her, Miss Marple reveals that Mrs. McGillicuddy had already reported the killer's features to the police on December 20th.

Miss Marple explains the motive: Campbell (identified as Comber) killed his wife, Anna Strandwasser, to marry the wealthy Emma without the risk of bigamy. He had hidden her body in a warehouse coffin and used various deceptions, including a fake identity and a postcard from Jamaica, to cover his tracks. He also attempted to poison the Crickenshaw family with small doses of arsenic to facilitate his greed. Miss Marple reveals she orchestrated the confrontation by tricking Campbell into believing Mrs. McGillicuddy had seen his face, when in reality, she had only seen his back. The passage concludes with Miss Marple expressing hope that Emma will find a better man than Campbell.
